title: Design System: Common Crawl Response Lint

display.purpose: The single page title (h1). One per report.

headline.purpose: Section headings (h2) with a 1px bottom rule.

body.purpose: All prose and table cells; page capped at 64rem.

label.purpose: Sub-headers (h4) and table column heads; uppercase, muted, functional.

mono.purpose: Machine literals only: note IDs, header names, raw sample values.

motion.name: marker-rotate
motion.purpose: Note-card disclosure marker (▸) rotates 90° on open.

motion.name: marker-rotate-reduced
motion.purpose: Honor prefers-reduced-motion: the rotation is inessential and may be dropped.

breakpoints.name: stat-grid
breakpoints.purpose: Header stat tiles reflow via auto-fit minmax, not media queries.

breakpoints.name: page-max
breakpoints.purpose: Body max-width; lines stay readable without a breakpoint system.

components.name: Severity Badge
components.description: Small uppercase chip naming an HTTP note's classification. Five variants: bad / warn / info / good / clean.
components.html: BAD WARN INFO GOOD CLEAN
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-block; padding: 0.1rem 0.4rem; border-radius: 4px; font: 600 0.7rem/1.25 system-ui, sans-serif; letter-spacing: 0.04em; border: 1px solid; } .ds-badge-bad { background: #fdecec; color: #a31515; border-color: #e9a0a0; } .ds-badge-warn { background: #fff8e1; color: #8a5400; border-color: #f0c356; } .ds-badge-info { background: #e7f0fb; color: #1b4079; border-color: #b6c9e3; } .ds-badge-good { background: #e6f4ea; color: #1e7c3a; border-color: #aed4ba; } .ds-badge-clean { background: #ececec; color: #555555; border-color: #c8c8c8; }

components.name: Note Card
components.description: Native details/summary disclosure per HTTP note. The 4px left edge encodes severity classification, not decoration.
components.html:
BADcache_control_syntax~1.2M sites8,431,902
Malformed Cache-Control directive observed in responses.
components.css: .ds-note { background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #e7e7ea; border-left: 4px solid #e9a0a0; border-radius: 6px; color: #1c1c1c; font: 400 15px/1.5 system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-note > summary { cursor: pointer; padding: 0.5rem 0.75rem; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 0.5rem; list-style: none; } .ds-note > summary::-webkit-details-marker { display: none; } .ds-note > summary::before { content: "\25b8"; color: #5f6168; transition: transform 0.15s; } .ds-note[open] > summary::before { transform: rotate(90deg); display: inline-block; } .ds-note-id { font-family: ui-monospace, 'SF Mono', Menlo, monospace; font-size: 0.9em; } .ds-note-sites { background: #f5f5f7; border: 1px solid #e7e7ea; border-radius: 4px; color: #5f6168; font-size: 0.75em; padding: 0.05rem 0.35rem; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } .ds-note-count { margin-left: auto; color: #5f6168; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } .ds-note-body { padding: 0.25rem 0.75rem 0.75rem; }

components.name: Run-Context Pill
components.description: Rounded-full chip carrying run metadata (label + tabular value). Warning variant flags a capped run.
components.html: CrawlCC-MAIN-2026-12 Records / WARC5,000
components.css: .ds-pill { background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #e7e7ea; border-radius: 999px; padding: 0.15rem 0.65rem; display: inline-flex; align-items: baseline; gap: 0.4rem; font-size: 0.82rem; color: #1c1c1c; } .ds-pill-label { color: #5f6168; font-size: 0.9em; } .ds-pill-value { font-weight: 600; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; } .ds-pill-warning { background: #fff8e1; border-color: #f0c356; color: #8a5400; }

components.name: Stat Tile
components.description: Plain header summary tile: muted uppercase label over a value. Deliberately NOT a hero metric.
components.css: .ds-stat { background: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #e7e7ea; border-radius: 8px; padding: 0.75rem 1rem; } .ds-stat dt { font-size: 0.8em; color: #5f6168; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.04em; } .ds-stat dd { margin: 0.25rem 0 0; font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: 600; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; }

components.name: Health Bar
components.description: Single horizontal stacked bar showing the severity mix at a glance. A proportion strip, not a gauge.
components.css: .ds-health { display: flex; width: 100%; height: 1.5rem; border-radius: 0.25rem; overflow: hidden; border: 1px solid #e7e7ea; } .ds-health-seg { display: block; height: 100%; }

narrative.overview: This is the rendered output of an instrument, not a product. ~123M HTTP responses go in; a ranked, scannable account of what they got right and wrong comes out. The design serves that reading the way a well-kept lab notebook serves an experiment: every figure carries its denominator, every elided long tail is annotated where it was cut, and the apparatus (shuffle caps, HLL estimates, top-K trims) is recorded in the margin rather than hidden. The page is flat by construction — not a single box-shadow — with depth carried by 1px borders, tonal row striping, and a restrained type scale. The one identity move is the five-level severity palette (bad / warn / info / good / clean), reading as clinical signal: precise instrument-panel readouts that rank and group, never alarm. It rejects the SaaS dashboard and the security-scanner scare sheet alike.
keyCharacteristics: Flat by construction — borders and tonal layering, zero shadows.
Density earned and spent on signal, never on decoration.
Severity as a sorting channel, carried by text + order as well as hue.
Honest apparatus: denominators, exclusions, and truncation always visible.
Dual-theme parity; system-ui type; tabular numerics throughout.

rules.name: The Meaning-Only Saturation Rule
rules.body: Saturated color is reserved for the functional blue and the five severity levels. Nothing decorative is allowed to be colorful. If a color appears, it classifies something.

rules.name: The Recede-on-Clean Rule
rules.body: The further a finding is from 'error', the quieter its color. Clean is gray on purpose; it must never compete with Bad for the eye.

rules.name: The System-Font Rule
rules.body: No web fonts, ever. The report renders in the reader's own UI font so it reads as an instrument's output. A loaded display face would make it a brochure.

rules.name: The Mono-Means-Literal Rule
rules.body: Monospace is reserved for machine literals (IDs, header names, raw values). Never use it for emphasis or headings.

rules.name: The No-Shadow Rule
rules.body: box-shadow is forbidden. If a surface needs to read as distinct, give it a border or a tonal step, not a shadow. If something looks like it's floating, the design has failed.

dos: Do keep the system flat — convey depth with 1px Border, the Canvas→Surface→Row-Alt tonal steps, and header underlines only.
Do reserve saturated color for the functional blue and the five severity levels; if a color appears, it must classify something.
Do carry severity in text and sort order, not hue alone, so the scale survives color-blindness and grayscale printing.
Do render figures with font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums and keep every aggregate's denominator and exclusions visible.
Do verify contrast in both light and dark schemes whenever you touch the palette: body ≥4.5:1, badges/segments ≥3:1 against their own ground.
Do use native details, table, and heading hierarchy; keep new sections semantic.
Do keep the report's HTML and Markdown twins in parity — a finding in one exists in the other.
donts: Don't build a SaaS dashboard: no hero-metric KPI cards, gauges, sparkline theater, or vanity numbers. The stat grid stays plain.
Don't build a security-scanner scare sheet: no red-everywhere alarmism, no letter grades, no naming-and-shaming sites. Measured over loud.
Don't add box-shadow anywhere. If a surface looks like it's floating, it's wrong.
Don't introduce a web font or a second proportional face; use monospace only for machine literals.
Don't use a colored border-left as decoration. The only sanctioned one is the 4px severity edge on note cards, and it encodes classification.
Don't let Clean compete with Bad for attention; the gray-on-purpose Clean level must recede.
Don't let the apparatus (shuffle caps, top-K trims, HLL math) become a headline; record it in the margin as a Muted footnote.
